name: dotted-dict

metadata.name: dotted-dict
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: dict object with support for addressing keys in dot notation.

documentation:
# dotted_dict

```{image} https://travis-ci.org/josh-paul/dotted_dict.svg?branch=master
:alt: Travis CI Build
:target: https://travis-ci.org/josh-paul/dotted_dict
```

```{image} http://codecov.io/github/josh-paul/dotted_dict/coverage.svg?branch=master
:alt: Coverage
:target: http://codecov.io/github/josh-paul/dotted_dict?branch=master
```

```{image} https://api.codeclimate.com/v1/badges/a5bdee8792e6a6379df1/maintainability
:alt: Code Climate Maintainability
:target: https://codeclimate.com/github/josh-paul/dotted_dict/maintainability
```

A light weight extension of the default python dict object. This allows for the use of key names as
object attributes.

Simple usage

```
In [1]: from dotted_dict import DottedDict

In [2]: example = DottedDict()

In [3]: example['foo'] = 1

In [4]: example.foo
Out[4]: 1

In [5]: example.bar = 2

In [6]: example
Out[6]: DottedDict({'bar': 2, 'foo': 1})

In [7]: del example['foo']

In [8]: del example.bar

In [9]: example
Out[9]: DottedDict({})
```

Also allows passing in of values in same manner as normal dict objects.

```
In [10]: example = DottedDict({'foo': 1, 'bar': 2})

In [11]: example
Out[11]: DottedDict({'bar': 2, 'foo': 1})
```

Issues with invalid characters. A valid key name in the scope of this library must conform to the
following regex {code}`[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*$`. In the case where your key name does not conform,
the library will mutate your key to a safe format. Spaces and invalid characters are replaced with
\_. In the case of the key beginning with an int, a leading _ is added.

```
In [12]: DottedDict({'My fun key': 1, 'John\'s': 1, 'Mr. Man': 1})
Out[12]: DottedDict({'John_s': 1, 'Mr__Man': 1, 'My_fun_key': 1})

In [13]: DottedDict({1: 2})
Out[13]: DottedDict({'_1': 2})
```

classes.name: dotted_dict.DottedDict

methods.name: dotted_dict.DottedDict.__repr__
documentation: Wrap the returned dict in DottedDict() on output.

methods.name: dotted_dict.DottedDict._is_valid_identifier_
documentation:
Test the key name for valid identifier status as considered by the python lexer. Also
check that the key name is not a python keyword.
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/12700893/how-to-check-if-a-string-is-a-valid-python-identifier-including-keyword-check

methods.name: dotted_dict.DottedDict._make_safe_
documentation: Replace the space characters on the key with _ to make valid attrs.

methods.name: dotted_dict.DottedDict._parse_input_
documentation: Parse the input item if dict into the dotted_dict constructor.

methods.name: dotted_dict.DottedDict.copy
documentation: Ensure copy object is DottedDict, not dict.
Override for the dict object to allow referencing of keys as attributes, i.e. dict.key

classes.name: dotted_dict.PreserveKeysDottedDict

methods.name: dotted_dict.PreserveKeysDottedDict.__repr__
documentation: Wrap the returned dict in DottedDict() on output.

methods.name: dotted_dict.PreserveKeysDottedDict._parse_input_
documentation: Parse the input item if dict into the dotted_dict constructor.

methods.name: dotted_dict.PreserveKeysDottedDict.copy
documentation: Ensure copy object is DottedDict, not dict.

Overrides auto correction of key names to safe attr names.  Can result in errors when using
attr name resolution.
